title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust
description: A cloned repository proposes hooks, includeIf config, credential helpers, aliases, filters, safe.directory exceptions, and a maintenance schedule on a shared build host. Learners cumulatively produce repository trust and local automation threat model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
